29 (instead of 28) if using Technical Mathematics rather than Mathematics.
28 with Mathematics, 29 with Technical Mathematics, 30 with Mathematical Literacy.

29 (instead of 28) with Technical Mathematics. CPUT's source brochure states a conditional trade-off here (e.g. "45% in Maths/Physical Sciences if the other is 50%+") that this app's requirement schema cannot fully express. Modelled here using the lower bound for both subjects as a reasonable approximation — borderline applicants should verify directly with CPUT.

29 (instead of 28) with Technical Mathematics. admissionRequirement: 27 (instead of 26) with Technical Mathematics. 27 with Technical Mathematics, 28 with Mathematical Literacy. Plus any other two subjects (excl. Life Orientation) at Level 3 (40%+).
31 (instead of 30) with Technical Mathematics. Plus any other two subjects (excl. Life Orientation) at Level 4 (50%+).
33 (instead of 32) with Technical Mathematics. Plus any two other subjects (excl. languages) each at minimum Level 3 (40–49%).

29 (instead of 28) with Technical Mathematics. CPUT's source brochure states a conditional trade-off here (e.g. "45% in Maths/Physical Sciences if the other is 50%+") that this app's requirement schema cannot fully express. 29 with Technical Mathematics, 30 with Mathematical Literacy.
27 (instead of 26) with Technical Mathematics. Alternatively 40%+ in Physical Sciences/Accounting/Business Studies/Economics/Geography (Level 3).
29 with Technical Mathematics, 30 with Mathematical Literacy. Foundation Programme route available (space permitting) with at least 40% in Life Sciences.
29 with Technical Mathematics, 30 with Mathematical Literacy. Foundation Programme route available (space permitting) with at least 40% in Life Sciences.
Requires English (4) AND one of: Mathematics (3), Mathematical Literacy (5), or Accounting (4). Mathematics and Accounting marks are each doubled in the APS calculation (Method 3).
Requires English (4) AND one of: Mathematics (3), Mathematical Literacy (4), or Accounting (3).
Applicants with Technical Mathematics at Level 5 (60%+) will also be considered.

26 required if using Mathematical Literacy instead of Mathematics. courseName: Bachelor of Education: Foundation Phase Teaching
admissionRequirement: Method not stated in the source brochure for this qualification — Method 1 (best 6, excl. Life Orientation) assumed. Also requires: an achievement rating of at least Level 4 in four NSC subjects overall (Bachelor's pass), with at least one of those four an official language enshrined in the Western Cape Language Policy (Afrikaans, English, isiXhosa), plus Level 3+ in another official SA language. Admission is competitive and not guaranteed even if minimums are met; selection also uses Grade 11 results provisionally.

courseName: Bachelor of Education: Intermediate Phase Teaching
admissionRequirement: Method not stated in the source brochure for this qualification — Method 1 (best 6, excl. Life Orientation) assumed. Also requires: an achievement rating of at least Level 4 in four NSC subjects overall (Bachelor's pass), with at least one of those four an official language enshrined in the Western Cape Language Policy (Afrikaans, English, isiXhosa), plus Level 3+ in another official SA language. Admission is competitive and not guaranteed even if minimums are met; selection also uses Grade 11 results provisionally. Plus any 2–3 of: Dramatic Art, Geography, History, Life Sciences, Music, Physical Sciences, Technology-related subjects, Visual Arts (each Level 4).

courseName: Bachelor of Education: Senior Phase and Further Education & Training Teaching
admissionRequirement: Method not stated in the source brochure for this qualification — Method 1 (best 6, excl. Life Orientation) assumed. Also requires: an achievement rating of at least Level 4 in four NSC subjects overall (Bachelor's pass), with at least one of those four an official language enshrined in the Western Cape Language Policy (Afrikaans, English, isiXhosa), plus Level 3+ in another official SA language. Admission is competitive and not guaranteed even if minimums are met; selection also uses Grade 11 results provisionally. Electives (choose from Mathematics, Mathematical Literacy, Accounting, Physical Sciences, Life Sciences) each require at least 50% (Level 4) at NSC level.
